Title: GbCYP86A1-1 from Gossypium barbadense positively regulates defence against Verticillium dahliae by cell wall modification and activation of immune pathways

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root barrier formation; control of water and nutrient transport; limits pathogen invasion and toxic compound entry; enhances defence against Verticillium dahliae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root periderm/peridermal cell wall (outer root cell wall)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes suberin accumulation in root cell walls, increases C16-C18 fatty acid content, and enhances resistance/tolerance to Verticillium dahliae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP86A1-1 was specifically expressed in roots and strongly induced by Verticillium dahliae, especially in resistant G. barbadense cv. Hai7124. 2. VIGS silencing of GbCYP86A1-1 in cotton markedly increased susceptibility to V. dahliae, indicating a positive role in defence. 3. Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis increased root suberin deposition, elevated C16-C18 fatty acids, reduced fungal invasion/biomass, and activated defence-associated pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of G. hirsutum acc. TM-1 tissues (BioProject PRJNA248163); RNA-seq of Arabidopsis roots on Illumina HiSeq2000 (accession NA); GC-based fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Verticillium dahliae strain V991 inoculation; water mock treatment
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies and directly forms suberin monomers required for target product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: C16-C18 fatty acids; fatty acyl-CoA derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes long-chain fatty acids/fatty acyl-CoAs to ω-hydroxy fatty acids and α,ω-dicarboxylic acids that serve as suberin monomers/precursors
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP86 family; fatty acid ω-hydroxylase; P450 domain with helix K, helix I and heme-binding domain; N-terminal transmembrane domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed in roots; induced after V. dahliae infection, highest induction in resistant cv. Hai7124
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown in cotton; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization by GFP colocalization; Sudan 7B staining of root sections; GC fatty acid analysis; fungal biomass qPCR; recovery assay; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among the three GbCYP86A1 homologs, GbCYP86A1-1 showed the strongest contribution to resistance. Overexpression also altered expression of lipid transport and immunity-related genes, but these are downstream responses rather than proof of direct regulation by the enzyme itself.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth and developmental differences were observed in transgenic Arabidopsis under the tested conditions; authors discuss possible growth–resistance trade-off in WT during infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering durable Verticillium resistance by enhancing root suberization; useful for transgenic deployment or stacking with other resistance genes in cotton breeding

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Root barrier formation; control of water and nutrient transport; pathogen defence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively contributes to Verticillium dahliae resistance, but weaker than GbCYP86A1-1; limited effect on root fatty acid/suberin traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP86A1-2 is root-expressed and induced by V. dahliae in cotton. 2. Silencing increased susceptibility to V. dahliae in cotton. 3. Overexpression in Arabidopsis improved resistance relative to WT but less than GbCYP86A1-1, with only modest lipid changes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq tissue expression reference PRJNA248163; GC-based fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Likely ω-hydroxy fatty acids and related oxidized fatty acid products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes ω-hydroxylation of fatty acids for suberin monomer formation, based on homology and family assignment
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP86 family fatty acid ω-hydroxylase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown in cotton; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overexpression caused higher C18:1 only, without broad C16-C18 increase or clear strong suberin phenotype; contribution to resistance is secondary to GbCYP86A1-1.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive allele/gene for improving resistance, but appears less effective than GbCYP86A1-1

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor positive role in cotton resistance when silenced, but overexpression in Arabidopsis did not significantly enhance Verticillium resistance or alter fatty acid content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP86A1-3 is root-expressed and induced by V. dahliae in cotton. 2. Silencing increased susceptibility in cotton. 3. Overexpression in Arabidopsis did not significantly differ from WT in resistance or major root lipid traits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq tissue expression reference PRJNA248163; GC-based fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely supplies precursor pool for suberin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Likely ω-hydroxy fatty acids and related oxidized fatty acid products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes ω-hydroxylation of fatty acids for suberin-related metabolism, inferred from homology
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP86 family fatty acid ω-hydroxylase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown in cotton; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional contribution appears weakest among the three homologs in the heterologous Arabidopsis assay.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty reported in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority candidate than GbCYP86A1-1 for metabolic engineering or breeding
